settings.title: Settings

settings.modelRouting.fallbackHint: If the primary model fails or is unavailable, the agent can switch to a backup model in the same family.
settings.transcribeModel.description: Audio transcription model. Audio is captured locally then sent to the backend for transcription.

settings.voiceOutput.description: Select the system voice that reads {{animalName}}'s responses.

settings.help.description: You can reopen the onboarding if you want to see the public capabilities again.
settings.security.hint: History of tool actions performed by the local agent.
settings.security.description: Everything the agent does is traced locally. You have the final say.

mail.title: Mail accounts
mail.descriptionPanel: Configure your IMAP/SMTP mailboxes. The agent can read, search, and reply via dedicated tools. Everything stays local — nothing is sent to the server.
mail.emptyState: No mailboxes. Click + Add to start.
mail.descriptionModal: Email address + password. Auto-detection handles IMAP/SMTP servers.
mail.labelIndexKb: Index this account in knowledge base (RAG)
mail.errorNoAutoConfig: No auto-configuration for {{domain}}. Set servers manually.
mail.hintGmailAppPassword: Gmail (and most Google Workspace accounts) reject normal passwords for IMAP. Generate an App Password at
mail.hintGmailNote: (16 characters, no spaces) then paste it below.
mail.hintKeychainPreserved: already in keychain, leave empty to keep
mail.hintAppPassword: Google requires an App Password (2FA / Workspace). Generate it at myaccount.google.com/apppasswords (16 characters, spaces ignored).
mail.hintGoogleAuth: Credentials rejected. Check: (1) it's a 16-character App Password, NOT your normal Google password; (2) IMAP enabled at mail.google.com/mail/u/0/#settings/fwdandpop; (3) "Less secure apps" access doesn't work, only App Password works with 2FA.
mail.hintGenericAuth: Credentials rejected by server. Check password (copy/paste without extra spaces) and that IMAP is enabled with your provider.
mail.hintConnectionFailed: Can't reach IMAP server. Check host/port/SSL or your network connection.
mail.footerKeychainNote: Password stored in OS keychain via local sidecar. No server transmission.
mail.confirmDelete: Delete account {{email}}? Local emails will be removed.

whatsapp.title: WhatsApp Conversations
whatsapp.description: Your personal chat keeps all capabilities. For each contact, you choose whether the assistant replies on your behalf and what role it plays.
whatsapp.noContacts: No conversations yet. Each contact who writes to you will appear here, agent disabled by default.
whatsapp.placeholderPersona: E.g., Reply on my behalf on this contact. Stay polite and brief, suggest a reminder if the request exceeds a simple reply.
whatsapp.hintThirdPartyLimit: Third-party contacts never get access to files, shell, or image generation.
whatsapp.hintDocFolder: Files in this folder will be offered to the bot and sent on request via WhatsApp.

kb.title: Embedding model
kb.confirmChangeModel: Changing the model destroys the vector library ({{chunks}} chunks). Documents remain intact and will be re-vectorized with {{modelId}}.
kb.hintPurgeUnindexed: Removes documents without any embedding (added before model config or with another model)

animalPicker.title: Animal
animalPicker.subtitle: Choose your personality. MonkeyAgentAgent is free; others unlock one at a time (€0.99).

persona.title: Professional persona
persona.subtitle: Specialize the agent for a trade. Restrict tools for focus, apply a dedicated skin. None = generalist mode (animal, all tools).

approval.waitingMessage: {{animalName}} awaits your approval before acting on shell, web, or files.
approval.label: Approval

onboarding.headline: Personal assistant, local-first
onboarding.description: Everything that follows stays on your desktop. The backend serves auth, billing, and model proxy — not your files, tasks, memory, or documents.
onboarding.card1.title: 1. Chat + model of choice
onboarding.card1.desc: Write or dictate. Choose your model (family then specific) directly in the input bar.
onboarding.card2.title: 2. Customize your animal
onboarding.card2.desc: {{name}} has a look, name, and preferred model. Switch skins (Vanilla, Coder, robot) whenever.
onboarding.card3.title: 3. Scheduled tasks
onboarding.card3.desc: Schedule a request for later with a pinned model. The agent runs it alone at the set time.
onboarding.card4.title: 4. Recurring + calendar
onboarding.card4.desc: Monthly calendar view, recurring tasks (RRULE), report or alert modes.
onboarding.card5.title: 5. Local library
onboarding.card5.desc: Drag files, notes, memories. Searchable memory and knowledge base — everything stays on your machine.
onboarding.card6.title: 6. WhatsApp bridge
onboarding.card6.desc: Optional WhatsApp bridge: same tools as desktop, replies from your phone.
onboarding.card7.title: 7. Integrations
onboarding.card7.desc: Gmail, Google Calendar, GitHub, macOS Shortcuts. Read/write with no intermediate server.
onboarding.card8.title: 8. Voice
onboarding.card8.desc: Mic dictation and TTS reading of responses. Works with local models.
onboarding.examples.title: Quick examples
onboarding.examples.content:
"Every Monday at 9am, summarize my unread emails."
"Read these two files and compare them."
"Find the latest report on X and brief it."
onboarding.trust.title: Trust rule
onboarding.trust.content: {{name}} asks before shell, file write, or sensitive web action. Plan + DoD audit before any `done`. You have the final say.

tasks.selectModelError: Please select a model to use an agent prompt.
tasks.reportTitle: When to send the report after the task runs: always, or only if a condition is met based on the result.
tasks.reportConditionPlaceholder: e.g. BTC dropped more than 5% in the last 24h
tasks.agentPromptPlaceholder: Prompt for the agent (leave empty for a shell command)
tasks.agentPromptExamplePlaceholder: e.g. Summarize unread emails and pick the 3 most urgent.

tour.title: Quick tips
tour.subtitle: 4 pointers to get started.
tour.tip1Body: The bar below accepts text, files, voice dictation.
tour.tip2Body: The selector at the bottom-left. Star = recommended to start.
tour.tip3Body: Your tasks, memory and documents — accessible in the top bar.
tour.tip4Body: Toggle at the top right of Settings to reveal advanced options.

faq.q1: Where does the model that answers my prompts actually run?
faq.a1: Either on your machine (Ollama, free, offline), or on another user's machine (P2P Provider, end-to-end encrypted via Noise protocol). No commercial cloud service (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter) is ever used. The Progsoft server never runs inference and never sees prompts.
faq.a2: On your machine, only. Conversations, memory, knowledge base, files, model weights → all local. The server only stores: account (email + hash), cosmetics inventory, temporary matchmaking metadata. No prompt, no response content.
faq.a3: Yes. No subscription, no top-ups, no paywall. Every feature, every model, every cosmetic is free. The app runs locally when possible and can route through other community providers when not.
faq.q4: How does the P2P provider network work?
faq.a4: When your machine can't run a model locally, the app finds another community member running that same model and routes your request to them through an end-to-end encrypted channel. You can opt in to share your own compute too — purely community, no money involved.
faq.a5: Yes — sidecar .ai.json manifest and embedded metadata (PNG tEXt / EXIF) for EU AI Act Art. 50(2) compliance. Always active on generated outputs, it's a legal requirement in the EU.
faq.q6: If I run as a Provider, do I see other users' prompts?
faq.a6: Technically yes, during inference — a model must read its input. The Provider runtime is a binary signed by Progsoft that refuses to log. A classifier (Llama Guard) refuses illegal content before returning. A local admin of your machine could still inspect — so only enable Provider mode if you accept that responsibility. For your own sensitive content, switch to local-only mode (Settings → Inference).

openaiConnector.title: OpenAI-compatible connector
openaiConnector.description: Expose an OpenAI-compatible /v1 endpoint on 127.0.0.1 so any external app (LangChain, LiteLLM, IDE plugins, curl scripts) can route through MonkeyAgentAgent: local llama → your other devices → friend providers. Bind is loopback-only, Bearer-auth required, no cloud fallback, prompts never logged.
openaiConnector.regenerateHint: Generate a new key and restart the connector

localModels.title: Local Models
localModels.description: Run on your machine — free, offline, private. Once installed, the model becomes an agent tool (prioritized over network calls).
localModels.noCatalog: No models in catalog (sidecar not started?).
